links.caveat: A NEGATIVE/diagnostic result: passing means the additive shared-eps bookkeeping works and the swelling branch demonstrably over-closes (degrades the fit), not that any physics is validated; the swelling branch carries mo2023_2's unvalidated fixed-dP parameters.
links.claim: Adding the parameter-free mo2023_2 swelling branch behaves as the framework predicts: the composite porosity closes below eps0 and the 9-bar Q(t) residual jumps from ~0.12 to ~0.65 g/s (worse than the flat null ~0.603), diagnosing that mo2023_2's fixed-dP fresh-grain swelling is mis-scaled for an already-swollen saturated pre-wet rig.
links.claim_not_supported: Does NOT show swelling improves or explains the 9-bar flow — the opposite: passing certifies the swelling branch makes the prediction worse and must not be tuned to hide the residual.
links.observable: Minimum composite porosity (eps_min vs eps0) and the 9-bar Q(t) RMSE vs the measured Waszkiewicz trace (g/s).

sources.note: physics/equations or model card; supplies no MANIFEST dataset for this gate

links.caveat: The (P_c,Q_c) calibration and dissolution sigmoid m_d(t) both come from the same rig/campaign as the 9-bar trace (m_d(t) = TDS x Q on that rig), so the reconstruction is not out-of-sample (soft circularity).
links.claim: In that extraction-only reduction the coupled model's predicted 9-bar Q(t) reproduces the measured Waszkiewicz 9-bar flow ramp with RMSE ~0.113 g/s (gate <0.13), matching the poroelastic component's own reconstruction (rung 4).
links.claim_not_supported: A passing RMSE is NOT independent validation of the kappa(t) mechanism; it is the same-rig reconstruction the poroelastic component already achieved, inherited unchanged.
links.observable: Mass flow Q(t) at 9 bar vs the measured Waszkiewicz trace over the 15-95 s saturated window (RMSE, g/s).

sources.note: physics/equations or model card; supplies no MANIFEST dataset for this gate

links.caveat: Establishes only that the code correctly reduces to a known special case; the datasets are inputs to that special case, not an out-of-sample test.
links.claim: With the swelling/compaction/fines-loss branches disabled, the coupled shared-porosity synthesis reduces exactly to its degenerate special case waszkiewicz2025.poroelastic (extraction-only Phi(t)=m_d(t)/m0 through the same poroelastic closure), adding no new behaviour in this limit.
links.claim_not_supported: Does NOT validate the full four-branch synthesis nor show the poroelastic physics is independently correct — only that the two models are indistinguishable when losses are off.
links.observable: Structural identity of the coupled model's extraction-only Phi(t)/Q(t) closure to the standalone poroelastic component (a code property).

sources.note: physics/equations or model card; supplies no MANIFEST dataset for this gate

links.caveat: Compared against a closed-form analytic solution, not an empirical dataset, and no docs/cards/ card exists for this component (in-module validation docstring only); the module's sphere-array checks show -7 to -8% error at toy radii, but this gate exercises only the exact channel case.
links.claim: The D3Q19 TRT lattice-Boltzmann permeability kernel reproduces the exact plane-Poiseuille channel permeability h^2/12 to within 0.5% (reported +0.03%).
links.claim_not_supported: Validates solver numerics against an analytic channel only — not against coffee/permeability data or sphere-drag at production resolution.
links.observable: Darcy permeability k of a plane-Poiseuille channel in lattice units vs the closed-form analytic value h^2/12.

sources.note: closed-form/analytic or code-internal check; no dataset, no card

links.caveat: Loads NO MANIFEST dataset — it runs a self-contained simulate_shot and builds the ceiling from model-internal constants (R0, bed_depth, C_S0, phi_s), so the ceiling is self-referential.
links.claim: The cameron2020 BDF two-population solver conserves solute mass (cup-mass EY agrees with solid-depletion EY to within 0.5 pp) and the predicted EY stays strictly below the model's own per-bed soluble-inventory ceiling.
links.claim_not_supported: Does NOT establish that the model's EY matches any measured espresso yield — it is a numerical mass-budget self-consistency check, not empirical validation.
links.observable: Extraction yield EY (%) via two internal accounting routes plus an inventory upper bound, all computed from the simulation and module constants.

sources.note: physics/equations or model card; supplies no MANIFEST dataset for this gate

links.caveat: Permeability k comes from kappa_fitted=1.196 (fitted to this same shot's flow) and the front is driven by this same shot's P(t); only the two porosity bracket endpoints are a-priori and the bracket is wide (~1.86x). REGISTRY LABELS THIS controlled_independent — the code does not support that tier; honest per-gate strength is sign_or_compatibility.
links.claim: A sharp-front infiltration prediction, driven by the DE1 shot's own measured pressure trace and a permeability from that shot's fitted kappa, yields a bed-saturation-time window (across an a-priori porosity bracket 0.173-0.322) that brackets the observed first-drip time (~7 s).
links.claim_not_supported: Does NOT demonstrate a parameter-free prediction independent of the evaluated data: k and P(t) are from the same shot, so 'parameter-free' holds only in that no parameter was tuned to hit the first-drip time itself.
links.conflict_note: Registry component tier is controlled_independent, but this gate's evidence is sign_or_compatibility on in-sample data (k from a kappa fitted to the same shot; front driven by that shot's own P(t); wide 1.86x porosity bracket). The 'independent' claim is not supported by the gate code.
links.observable: First-liquid / bed-saturation time (s): observed = time cup weight first exceeds 0.5 g; predicted = t_saturate when the modelled front reaches bed depth L, at porosity endpoints 0.173 and 0.322.

sources.note: physics/equations or model card; supplies no MANIFEST dataset for this gate
physics/equations or model card; supplies no MANIFEST dataset for this gate

links.caveat: Fig 15 is the paper's OWN model output (manifest: 'model-reproduced curve ... not pixel-traced'), and the Table I fine-grind params driving solve() are the authors' fitted values transcribed into a hardcoded dataclass, not loaded from data.
links.claim: The re-implemented Foster machine-mode ODE (pump characteristic + trapped-air headspace) reproduces the paper's own Fig 15 normalized bed-flow curve, dipping to Q/Qm~0.181 at t~2.0 s and recovering, to RMSE <0.01 over the t_p..t_s window.
links.claim_not_supported: It does not validate the mid-shot flow minimum against any measured flow trace, nor establish that pump+headspace dynamics cause real early-shot flow dips (CT validation is a separate gate).
links.observable: Normalized bed flow Q/Qm = min(Q_p, f(H,s))/Q_m vs experiment time.

links.caveat: The parameters (K/k, phi_T, t_shift) are author-fitted and the two target numbers are the authors' own reported model outputs; the Fig-15 flow-minimum shape validation is deferred.
links.claim: The staged pump/headspace machine-mode ODE, run from the transcribed Table I/II parameters and offset by the fitted t_shift=0.796 s, reproduces the authors' reported ponding time t_p=0.823 s and saturation time t_s=6.669 s.
links.claim_not_supported: Not a test against independent experimental first-drip/CT timing — it reproduces the paper's own reported model-output numbers from the paper's own fitted parameters.
links.observable: Two scalar times (s): ponding time t_p and saturation time t_s, model-computed vs the authors' reported values.

links.caveat: The refit output (0.2186) is compared only to the card's constant 0.215 — same digitized data, a ~42-of-99-point subset of a FULL-IMMERSION study, not flow/espresso extraction.
links.claim: Re-fitting the lumped equilibrium product K*E_max via Eq. 11 to the digitized Fig. 3 TDS-vs-brew-ratio data (R_brew>=3) recovers ~0.215, the card's reported value.
links.claim_not_supported: Does NOT validate the desorption model against independent data or for espresso/flow — only that the re-estimate reproduces the paper's reported value from the same curve.
links.observable: The dimensionless equilibrium desorption product K*E_max from equilibrium TDS vs brew ratio R_brew for full-immersion brewing.

links.caveat: The matched ratio is the paper's SIMULATED curve, not measurement (manifest: 'verification (MODEL output, not data) ... the paper's headline claim rests on ZERO data here'); D_W=1.25e-10 is a code constant fitted to a ~30 s timescale.
links.claim: The re-implemented swelling-PDE -> Eq.21 bed-porosity -> Carman-Kozeny chain reproduces the paper's SIMULATED Fig 3(a) fixed-dp flow-decay ratio q(60s)/q(0) for all four powders within 20%, preserves the coarser-throttles-less ordering E<H<M<F, and matches the closed-form max swelling s_m=3.57%.
links.claim_not_supported: It does not test the swelling-throttled-flow mechanism against any measured flow-decay data; the ordering is a reproduced property of the source's model, not an experimentally confirmed fact.
links.observable: Fixed-dp flow-decay ratio q(60s)/q(0) (dp/mu/L cancelled) per powder E/H/M/F, plus the scalar s_m.

links.caveat: No empirical yield data is compared — the gate checks a self-consistency property of the implemented model against the paper's Fig. 2 (itself model output); granulometry Table 1 enters as a model input, not a fit/eval target; the swelling magnitude C_M is assumed.
links.claim: At fixed flow the implemented mo2023_2 two-population model gives nearly identical yield with swelling on vs off (max relative difference <5%) because swelling raises grain porosity/D_p (~+15%) but grows R^2 (~+7%) and these offset — reproducing the paper's Fig. 2 model behaviour.
links.claim_not_supported: Does NOT validate the swelling mechanism or absolute yields against experiment — only that the code exhibits the offsetting-timescale insensitivity the paper asserts at fixed flow.
links.observable: End-of-shot yield (fraction of extractable inventory) with swelling on vs off at q=2/3/4 mL/s (fixed-q), contrasted with the fixed-dP Carman-Kozeny flow-decay ratio.

links.caveat: The Table 2 parameters were fitted by the authors to the SAME experimental_kinetics being reproduced; the authors note the params lack generality and the gate uses a centre-grind approximation.
links.claim: Run with the authors' Table 2 per-solute fitted parameters, the two-grain multi-solute PDE solver reproduces the authors' own published fit MAPEs against the Schmieder kinetics (TDS 6.07, caffeine 4.59, trigonelline 7.85, CGA 4.98 %, each within ~3.5 pts).
links.claim_not_supported: Does NOT demonstrate predictive accuracy on any independent/out-of-sample data — fit and evaluation are the same data.
links.observable: Per-solute MAPE (%) between simulated and measured fraction concentrations over 15 experiments x 6 time-fraction windows.

links.caveat: 'Parameter-free' holds in that the medium-MW Deff was measured independently at the particle scale (Table 4.9) and not fitted to these bed yields — but the gate runs NO solver; it loads pre-digitized MPE numbers from the thesis figure and checks a threshold.
links.claim: Across all 15 digitized Fig. 7.4 espresso conditions the thesis's reported medium-MW (non-fitted microstructural Deff) bed-scale prediction error is MPE <=14.3%, and the medium-MW single-Deff column has a lower mean MPE than the low- or high-MW columns.
links.claim_not_supported: Does NOT reproduce the yields from any puckworks solver and does NOT test an independent lab/campaign — all data are from the same thesis and apparatus family.
links.observable: Mean percentage error (MPE, %) between the thesis's predicted and measured espresso yields per molecular-weight Deff class, digitized from Fig. 7.4 (~+/-0.5 pp).

sources.note: physics/equations or model card; supplies no MANIFEST dataset for this gate

links.caveat: Neither dataset reaches bulk espresso TDS (khomyakov >=15 wt% solids, TR2001 fit on a 51-Brix industrial extract), so both extrapolate toward water for espresso; the gate records the offset, it does not adjudicate which source is correct.
links.claim: Two independent measured coffee-extract viscosity datasets (khomyakov2020 kinematic converted to dynamic mu=rho*nu vs telisromero2001 Eq-10 closure) agree in sign/direction across their Xw 76-90% / T 295-365 K overlap but khomyakov runs systematically ~+37% (median 25-50%, max <60%) above TR2001.
links.claim_not_supported: Does NOT establish which source is correct nor validate either at espresso TDS — only records a bounded, systematic inter-source magnitude discrepancy over the measured overlap box.
links.conflict_note: khomyakov2020's measured kinematic viscosity (converted via rho*nu) runs systematically ~+37% ABOVE the telisromero2001 Eq-10 closure across the Xw 76-90% / T 295-365 K overlap (median 25-50%, max <60%) — a bounded material/instrument offset (Russian production extract vs Brazilian 51-Brix batch) agreeing in sign but quantifying the G10 absolute-magnitude uncertainty at concentrated in-pore strengths.
links.observable: Per-cell percent offset (mu_khomyakov - mu_TR2001)/mu_TR2001 over the overlapping concentration/temperature cells, summarized by median/min/max and sign.

sources.note: physics/equations or model card; supplies no MANIFEST dataset for this gate

links.caveat: No espresso-TDS rheology is measured on file; the value extrapolates the Telis-Romero fit toward the pure-water limit, and espresso colloids (oils, CO2, melanoidin foam) could raise apparent viscosity beyond the smooth extrapolation.
links.claim: The transcribed reference espresso viscosity estimate (~4-6e-4 Pa.s, ~1.3-1.9x pure water 3.15e-4 Pa.s) exceeds pure water, stays within 1.0-2.5x, and lies in the Newtonian band (espresso 4-12% solids is below the ~36%-solids power-law onset).
links.claim_not_supported: Does NOT measure or validate espresso-liquor viscosity — only that a transcribed, extrapolated reference value is above water and within a Newtonian bound.
links.conflict_note: The envelope estimate this gate checks (~1.3-1.9x water, 4-6e-4 Pa.s) conflicts with the Telis-Romero-2001 Eq-10 closure reconstruction (gate_g10_telisromero_closure), which puts BULK shot-TDS mu at only ~1.06x water and reassigns the ~1.3-2x magnitude to concentrated early in-pore liquor (Xw~76%), not bulk shot TDS.
links.observable: Reference espresso dynamic-viscosity ratio to pure water at brew temperature, plus Newtonian-band membership.

links.caveat: It reproduces the source's own figure-read endpoints for a dilute industrial extract and bundles code-property checks (typo diagnostic, alpha offset, normalization guard); espresso T/concentration are above/at the source's edge.
links.claim: The transcribed Telis-Romero 2000 linear thermophysical closures (rho/cp/k/alpha) reproduce the card's figure-endpoint / water-limit anchors within plotting accuracy, the typo-corrected Eq(6) alpha coefficient reproduces the Fig-5 endpoint while the printed one does not, and the fraction-vs-percent guard raises on percent input.
links.claim_not_supported: It does NOT independently validate coffee-extract thermophysical properties (no external measurement) and says nothing about viscosity or flow — it confirms faithful transcription and flags the Eq-6 typo.
links.conflict_note: Supplies the density used to convert local concentration to X_w in the mu(c,T) study (bulk rho ~1003, <=1% over water; first-drip rho ~1196); the viscosity magnitude tensions live in the companion 2001/bulk gates.
links.observable: Density rho, specific heat cp, thermal conductivity k and thermal diffusivity alpha of soluble-coffee extract vs T and water fraction (plus a unit guard).

sources.note: physics/equations or model card; supplies no MANIFEST dataset for this gate

links.caveat: It reproduces the source's own dilute industrial soluble-coffee extract (X_w 76-90%, one 51-Brix batch) with the authors' own fitted closures; espresso TDS sits at/below the dilute edge, so any espresso application is extrapolation.
links.claim: The independently transcribed Telis-Romero 2001 Eq(10)/(13) viscosity closures reproduce the full digitized Table 1 (24 eta cells) and Table 2 (27 K cells) at about the authors' own reported fit error, cross-checking the digitization against the transcribed coefficients.
links.claim_not_supported: It does NOT validate espresso-liquor viscosity nor independently confirm the closures against another lab/coffee — it only shows the two transcription paths of the SAME paper are mutually consistent.
links.conflict_note: Anchors the MEASURED eta grid that yields ~1.06x-water bulk-shot viscosity, undercutting the paywalled-snippet envelope's ~1.3-2x guess; it does not reconcile the separate khomyakov2020 ~+37%-above-TR2001 offset.
links.observable: Newtonian dynamic viscosity eta (Pa.s) and power-law consistency index K (Pa.s^n) of soluble-coffee extract vs temperature and water fraction.

sources.note: physics/equations or model card; supplies no MANIFEST dataset for this gate

links.caveat: The negligibility conclusion is derived from a MODEL (cameron2020's concentration field) fed measured mu, with permeability/pressure held fixed and no measured espresso flow to test against; a bounded compatibility result that excludes genuinely saturating (choked/ristretto) shots.
links.claim: Driving cameron2020's own in-pore liquor concentration field through the MEASURED Telis-Romero 2001 eta(c,T) grid, the in-pore liquor never approaches saturation (peak mu < 1.15x water), so the constant-water-viscosity assumption used by every registered flow model gives <8% outlet flow suppression and >0.95 depth-averaged flow factor at espresso conditions.
links.claim_not_supported: It does NOT empirically demonstrate real espresso flow is insensitive to liquor viscosity nor validate cameron2020's concentration field — only that IF that field is right, the measured mu(c,T) correction is small.
links.conflict_note: Resolves the core G10 magnitude tension for BULK flow — measured TR2001 mu gives ~1.06x-water (negligible), refuting the ~1.3-2x snippet envelope for shot TDS — and is claimed robust even under the khomyakov2020 ~+37% offset.
links.observable: Darcy flow-suppression factor (mu_water / mu_local) implied by concentration-dependent liquor viscosity over a simulated shot's in-pore concentration field.

sources.note: physics/equations or model card; supplies no MANIFEST dataset for this gate
physics/equations or model card; supplies no MANIFEST dataset for this gate

links.caveat: Explicitly a consistency/bounds check, not validation: the Ulka envelope is manufacturer catalogue ENDPOINTS only (+/-15%, cold water, 120V/60Hz) with a concave-droop (non-quadratic) shape, and the real DE1 Q(P) is closed firmware.
links.claim: The operative measured single-rig Waszkiewicz brewer quadratic (dP=aQ^2+bQ+c) stays inside the Ulka manufacturer envelope over the working flow band (dP below the 15-bar deadhead, free-flow > 1.90 g/s) — a bounds consistency check between two on-disk datasets.
links.claim_not_supported: It does NOT establish the quadratic is the correct pump physics or holds outside the measured band (the true vibe-pump curve is concave), nor provide an independent DE1-specific characteristic.
links.observable: Pump/basket pressure drop dP (bar) vs flow Q over the working range, bounded against manufacturer free-flow debit and deadhead endpoints.

links.caveat: kappa=1.196 was fitted to this same DE1 fixture-A trace (Fo_F then computed on it, nothing held out); k_I is a ceramics fit never calibrated on coffee and DE1 k~7e-15 sits below that fit's support (explicit extrapolation); the untamped band it beats is a hardcoded card number.
links.claim: Computing Fo_F along the real DE1 fixture-A tamped trace (Darcy k from the fitted kappa=1.196 via the kappa->SI chain, k_I from the ceramics-fitted Zhou closure) yields a peak Fo_F that is O(1), falls inside the backlog's 0.3-0.9 gusher-regime estimate, and exceeds the card's untamped espresso ceiling (0.0639) by >10x.
links.claim_not_supported: It does not empirically validate that espresso flow is inertial nor measure Fo_F; only that the model's computed Fo_F on one uncontrolled community fixture is compatible with the backlog's higher estimate.
links.conflict_note: Registry-internal source conflict: the wadsworth paper's own worked espresso band (Fo 0.0161-0.0639) vs the backlog's gusher-regime estimate (Fo ~0.3-0.9); the audit adjudicates toward the backlog's higher value.
links.observable: Peak Forchheimer number Fo_F = rho*k*q/(mu*k_I) evaluated along the recorded trace.

sources.note: physics/equations or model card; supplies no MANIFEST dataset for this gate
physics/equations or model card; supplies no MANIFEST dataset for this gate
physics/equations or model card; supplies no MANIFEST dataset for this gate

links.caveat: Untamped beds only; the collapse constants (alpha=4808/m, exponent 4.4) are the authors' own fit applied back to their own Table 1, so this reproduces their published collapse; a Table-1 erratum and a grindmap DOI/beta discrepancy are flagged in the manifest.
links.claim: The paper's percolation permeability form k = k_star(phi_p,s_p)*phi_p^4.4 collapses their own Table 1 measured permeabilities to a geometric-mean ratio near 0.9 (gate window 0.7 < GM < 1.2).
links.claim_not_supported: Does NOT independently validate k(R,phi) against out-of-sample or tamped-bed permeability data.
links.observable: Dimensionless collapse ratio k_measured / (k_star(phi_p,s_p)*phi_p^4.4) across the Table 1 grind rows, aggregated as a geometric mean.

sources.note: physics/equations or model card; supplies no MANIFEST dataset for this gate

links.caveat: Soft circularity: m_d(t) is derived from TDS(t) x Q(t) on the same rig and the static calibration is from the same campaign, so the reproduction is not out-of-sample; the first ~15 s transient is skipped and the authors give no source error metric (semi-quantitative).
links.claim: With zero extra free parameters (static (P_c,Q_c) plus the dissolution sigmoid Phi(t)=m_d(t)/m0), Eq. 18 reproduces the measured 9-bar Q(t) ramp semi-quantitatively — long-run mean within 10% and correlation >0.90 over t>=15 s.
links.claim_not_supported: A passing correlation does NOT show the mechanism is predictive on a new pressure/rig/coffee, nor that dissolution (rather than swelling, per the authors' own CT) drives the change.
links.observable: Mass flow Q(t) at 9 bar vs time (long-run mean g/s and Pearson correlation, t>=15 s).

links.caveat: The refit target (12 bar, 1.90 g/s) IS the published static calibration and both are fit to the same within-rig equilibrium curve; P_c=12.4+/-3.0 bar is weakly constrained (~25%).
links.claim: Refitting the poroelastic equilibrium closure (Eq. 16) to the 11-pressure long-run pressure-flow curve recovers the calibration pair (P_c,Q_c) ~ (12 bar, 1.90 g/s) and reproduces the paper's ingested static calibration to <0.05 bar / <0.01 g/s.
links.claim_not_supported: Does NOT independently validate the poroelastic model or transfer to another rig/coffee/grind — only confirms the refit reproduces the authors' own calibration on their own data.
links.observable: Steady-state basket-pressure vs mass-flow curve; recovered calibration parameters P_c, Q_c.
